changeReason: Improved translation from Japanese. Old: "Sending the Brat Back"

TextENGNew: It had been three days since Satoko had taken to bed with her supposed fever.
changeReason: Old says 'since he told her to stay home' which is fabricated; Japanese states Satoko took to bed with fever.

TextENGNew: ...That annoying teacher Chie had called again. He screamed at her and slammed down the phone.
changeReason: New translation adds the teacher's name 'Chie' (JPN 知恵), which was omitted in the original.

TextENGNew: But that woman was persistent.
She would not let this go.
Worse, she might raise some ugly suspicions and get the police involved.
changeReason: Old fabricates content about Teppei's fear of Satoko seeking help at school.

TextENGNew: ...He had heard from someone that the consultation center sometimes brought the police along.
Teppei had plenty of skeletons in his closet.
changeReason: Old uses 'weight on his shoulders' (burden); new uses 'skeletons in his closet,' correctly capturing the JPN idiom about having a criminal past to hide.

TextENGNew: If the police got involved, it would inevitably lead to his other shady business.@k That was the one thing he absolutely could not allow.
If only he had the money.
If he could just find the bankbook his brother and sister-in-law left behind, he could get out of this suffocating village.
changeReason: Old says only 'brother's bankbook'; new correctly includes 'brother and sister-in-law' (JPN 兄夫婦).

TextENGNew: Teppei generally stayed indoors, finding the stares of the other villagers unpleasant.
To pass the time, he would invite his friends over to play mahjong.
changeReason: Old inaccurately adds 'at Satoko's house' and misrepresents causality; JPN says he invited friends over to pass the time.

TextENGNew: But mahjong requires four players.
When he could not gather enough people, he had nothing to do but sit around watching television.
So he began spending his ample free time on his treasure hunt.
He was going to leave this house eventually anyway.
changeReason: Old says 'Satoko would end up cleaning the mess' but Japanese says 'He was going to leave this house eventually.'

TextENGNew: What did he care if some furniture got broken?
changeReason: Old says 'it wasn't his home anyway' but Japanese says 'What did he care if some furniture got broken.'

TextENGNew: Teppei pulled everything out of the closets and pried open ceiling panels.
changeReason: Old has displaced content; Japanese describes Teppei pulling things from closets and prying ceiling panels.

TextENGNew: He yanked every drawer from every dresser, dumped out the contents, and inspected the empty compartments.
changeReason: Old mentions 'closet' and 'attic' not in the JPN; the JPN describes pulling drawers from dressers and dumping contents.

TextENGNew: A miser like his wife would have been clever about hiding a stash.
changeReason: Old drops key details; the JPN specifically says 'a miser' is 'clever at hiding a stash,' both omitted from the original.

TextENGNew: He would not find it without being this thorough.
changeReason: Old says 'she must have hid it somewhere unusual' but Japanese says 'wouldn't find it without being thorough.'

TextENGNew: He continued ransacking the house in this fashion until--
changeReason: Old adds fabricated content about floors and Satoko tackling him.

TextENGNew: ``N-Not Nii-nii's room...!!!``
``What the hell do you think you're doing?!!``
``No! Please don't go in Nii-nii's room...!!!``
At first, Teppei took Satoko's extreme reaction as proof that the bankbook was hidden in that room.
Teppei shoved Satoko aside and tried to force his way into Satoshi's room.
changeReason: Old says he 'forced his way inside' (implying success); new correctly says 'tried to force his way' (JPN indicates attempt, not completion), and adds 'Satoshi's room.'

TextENGNew: ...But Satoko fought back desperately to stop him.
``...Hic...! That room... belongs to Nii-nii...@k@vS20/04/440400097. If he comes home and finds it ruined... Nii-nii will be devastated...@k@vS20/04/440400098. *sob*... *hic*...!!``
``What are you going on about? I'm not going to wreck it just because it's your brother's room. You don't even know if Satoshi is alive or dead.``
changeReason: New adds 'alive or dead' matching JPN, and includes Satoshi's name. Old also drops Teppei's dismissive preamble.

TextENGNew: ``Nii-nii is alive!! He will come home!!@k@vS20/04/440400100. Waaaaaaaah!!@k@vS20/04/440400101. Don't destroy his room!!``

``...Calm down. I'm not going to destroy it.@k@vS20/21/442000015. This room, right?@k@vS20/21/442000016. I just want to give it a little cleaning.@k@vS20/21/442000017. There's no reason to keep a room preserved for someone who's not coming back.@k@vS20/21/442000018. I'm just going to tidy up a bit.``
changeReason: New captures full JPN dialogue including 'no reason to keep a room for someone who's not coming back,' which old truncated.

TextENGNew: But Satoko still resisted.@k Even Teppei decided it was better to back off for now.
changeReason: Original translation was empty; new translation provides content.

TextENGNew: ``All right, all right!! If you're a good girl, I won't go in this room.@k@vS20/21/442000020. Okay?@k@vS20/21/442000021. Deal?``
Satoko's reaction only confirmed it for Teppei.@k The bankbook had to be in that room.
changeReason: Original translation was empty; new translation provides content.

TextENGNew: ...But if Satoko detected even the slightest sign that he had tampered with the room, she would become unmanageable.
changeReason: Old vaguely says 'Satoko would lose it if he went inside'; new correctly captures the specific condition about detecting traces of tampering.

TextENGNew: Come to think of it... it would actually be better to send Satoko to school.
changeReason: Original translation was empty; new translation provides content.

TextENGNew: While she was at school, he could search Satoshi's room in secret.
``Since you've been such a good girl, your cold must be all better by now.@k@vS20/21/442000023. Starting tomorrow, you go back to school.``
``......Thank you so much... thank you so much...``
He had trained her to say thank you whenever he granted her anything, but hearing it repeated over and over was unsettling.
changeReason: Old only captures that he trained her to say thank you; new includes the full meaning that hearing it repeated was unsettling.

TextENGNew: Teppei, being the selfish man he was, found even that irritating.
changeReason: Old repeats content from the previous line; new correctly translates the JPN as Teppei selfishly finding it irritating.

TextENGNew: ``Get out of my sight already, you're getting on my nerves, honestly... ...Aaagh!@k@vS20/21/442000025. So damn annoying!!``
